channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Centre asks West Bengal to accelerate PM-JAY implementation, HPV vaccination and TB elimination efforts
item.description: The Union government has urged West Bengal to speed up implementation of major public health programmes, including Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY, HPV vaccination and tuberculosis elimination initiatives. Dur...

item.title: Durban swimmers brave rough seas during Oceans 8 Charity Swim for KwaZulu-Natal charities
item.description: Swimmers gathered at Vetch’s Beach in Durban on Saturday for the start of the 2026 Oceans 8 Charity Swim, an annual open-water event aimed at raising funds for non-profit organisations operating in Kw...

item.title: A Look at Marianne Faithfull’s Transformation in the 1970s Through Portraits
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 30 portrait photographs of Marianne Faithfull taken during the 1970s, a decade that marked one of the most dramatic transformations in her li...

item.title: Collingwood forward Jamie Elliott ruled out with ACL rupture after West Coast clash
item.description: Collingwood Football Club forward Jamie Elliott has been confirmed to have suffered a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in his knee, an injury that is expected to sideline him for approximatel...

item.title: Filipino workers discuss factors that drive job satisfaction in the workplace
item.description: In a business report published by ABS-CBN News, Filipino workers shared their insights on what contributes to job satisfaction in today’s workplace. The article highlights how employees in the Philipp...

item.title: Djokovic emerges as potential disruptor to Sinner’s French Open dominance amid depleted field
item.description: The article examines the shifting dynamics heading into the French Open, focusing on whether Novak Djokovic can realistically challenge the current dominance of Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz. In re...

item.title: Questions raised over coordination failures between Australian security agencies in Bondi terror case
item.description: The Albanese Government has faced scrutiny after declining to directly address reports suggesting a breakdown in communication between Australia’s national security agencies in relation to the accused...

item.title: South Africa diesel dependency exposed amid Iran-related oil shock
item.description: The article highlights how the Iran-related oil shock is exposing deep structural weaknesses in South Africa’s energy and logistics systems, especially its heavy dependence on diesel. While many peopl...

item.title: A Look Back at Mary Tyler Moore’s Career and Cultural Influence in the 1970s
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday highlights the life, career, and cultural impact of actress and producer Mary Tyler Moore during the 1970s, accompanied by a collection of 40 photographs from the dec...

item.title: Police launch nationwide manhunt after armed robbery at Naledi Pandor’s residence
item.description: South African Police Service (SAPS) has launched an urgent manhunt following an armed robbery and housebreaking at the home of former cabinet minister Naledi Pandor. According to police reports, suspe...

item.title: French Open draw timing, seeded players, wildcards and full entry list explained
item.description: The French Open is set to begin at Roland-Garros, marking the culmination of the clay-court season, with anticipation building around both the men’s and women’s draws. The official draw is scheduled f...

item.title: Faisalabad TikToker arrested over obscene video targeting female tourists; separate sexual violence case reported
item.description: A TikToker from Faisalabad has been arrested after allegedly posting a video containing obscene language about female tourists at Lake Saif-ul-Muluk in the Naran area. According to reports, the accuse...

item.title: Study finds international media focused mainly on Tigray conflict while underreporting violence in other Ethiopian regions
item.description: A new academic study examined how major international media organisations reported Ethiopia’s conflicts between January 2020 and March 2025. The researchers analysed 1,412 news reports from BBC, CNN, ...

item.title: CSL shares tumble as profit downgrades and rising competition erode investor confidence
item.description: CSL, one of Australia’s largest and most widely held healthcare companies, has suffered a significant fall in its share price, wiping close to $10 billion from its market value. The decline was trigge...

item.title: Janine Berdin gains international recognition from Demi Lovato, Doechii, and Lauren Jauregui
item.description: Filipino singer Janine Berdin has recently gained international attention after being noticed by globally recognised music artists Demi Lovato, Doechii, and Lauren Jauregui. The development highlights...

item.title: Report finds ongoing compliance and risk control issues at Westpac’s small business banking division
item.description: A recent Financial Review opinion piece highlights ongoing concerns about governance and risk management within Westpac, particularly in its small business banking division. The article argues that de...

item.title: Jim Acosta Demonstrates Cognitive Assessment Cited in Political Debate Over Trump’s Claims
item.description: Journalist Jim Acosta participated in a televised demonstration of a cognitive assessment that former President Donald Trump has publicly claimed to have taken and passed multiple times. The exercise ...

item.title: Reactions as Ruth Odinga attends wedding in Limuru with husband Nana Busia
item.description: Kisumu Woman Representative Ruth Odinga recently attended a wedding ceremony in Limuru alongside her husband, Dr Nana Busia, where they joined friends, family, and dignitaries to celebrate the union o...

item.title: Mbeki urges African leaders to prioritise poverty eradication and self-reliance ahead of Africa Day
item.description: Former South African president Thabo Mbeki used the 16th Thabo Mbeki Africa Day Lecture in Cape Town to highlight poverty as one of Africa’s most urgent and persistent challenges. Speaking ahead of Af...

item.title: Experts report rising anxiety diagnoses among children and youth across Canada
item.description: Mental health experts are reporting a significant rise in anxiety diagnoses among children and young people, raising concerns about the long-term well-being of youth in Canada. According to specialist...
